meta.title: ASM Withdrawal after Epilepsy Surgery (WAMS)
meta.cff_info:
WAMS — ASM withdrawal after epilepsy surgery

Ferreira-Atuesta C, de Tisi J, McEvoy AW, … Galovic M. Brain 2023;146(6):2389–2398.

DOI: 10.1093/brain/awac437 · official tool: predictepilepsy.github.io

Model

Cox proportional-hazards model of seizure recurrence other than focal non-motor aware seizures after starting antiseizure-medication (ASM) withdrawal following resective epilepsy surgery in adults. Predictors and adjusted hazard ratios (Table 1):

  • Focal non-motor aware seizures after surgery & before withdrawal: aHR 5.53 — strongest predictor
  • Time from surgery to start of withdrawal: aHR 0.90 per year (longer wait → lower risk)
  • FBTCS before surgery: aHR 1.60
  • Number of ASMs at time of surgery: aHR 1.24 each

Curves use the published Brain-2023 coefficients with the baseline seizure-free survival estimated from the WAMS2 registry (n=1072, 333 events; overall 80%/72% seizure-free at 2/4 years, matching the paper). COSY(t) = chance of a seizure in the next 12 months given seizure-free at month t. For clinical use see the official WAMS tool.
meta.authors: Ferreira-Atuesta C, de Tisi J, McEvoy AW, et al.
meta.clinical_utility: Individualised probability of seizure recurrence (other than focal non-motor aware seizures) after starting ASM withdrawal following epilepsy surgery, with cumulative-risk and COSY (chance of seizure in the next year) curves. This calculator reproduces the official WAMS web app (asmwithdrawal.shinyapps.io/outcome — 'freedom from disabling seizures'): its coefficients, baseline and 95% CI were aligned to that app so the two agree.
meta.outcome: Predictive models for starting antiseizure medication withdrawal following epilepsy surgery in adults

inputs.name: Focal non-motor aware seizures after surgery
inputs.info: Focal non-motor aware seizures (auras) occurring after surgery and before starting ASM withdrawal. The strongest predictor of seizure recurrence (aHR 5.53).

inputs.name: Time from surgery to start of withdrawal
inputs.info: Years between epilepsy surgery and the start of ASM withdrawal. Each additional year lowers the hazard (aHR 0.90/year).

inputs.name: FBTCS before surgery
inputs.info: History of focal-to-bilateral tonic-clonic seizures before surgery (aHR 1.60).

inputs.name: Number of ASMs at time of surgery
inputs.info: Number of antiseizure medications the patient was taking at the time of surgery (aHR 1.24 per ASM).

provenance.notes: Aligned 2026-07-07 to the official WAMS app (predictepilepsy.github.io -> asmwithdrawal.shinyapps.io/outcome). Coefficients reverse-engineered from the app (auras HR4.76/gtcs1.41/asms1.41/time0.942), baseline = app reference curve, 95% CI approximated from the app's CIs (cll-scale, per-predictor variance increments). NOTE: the app matches neither the paper's published aHRs nor a standard Cox re-fit of WAMS2_registry (see form46_registry_alt).
